title: Atalantia lauterbachii
taxonomy: Atalantia lauterbachii is classified within the family Rutaceae and the genus Atalantia. The family Rutaceae is commonly known as the citrus family, which includes a variety of plants such as citrus fruits and herbs. The genus Atalantia is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands. Atalantia lauterbachii is specifically native to the Marshall Islands in the Pacific Ocean. This classification places Atalantia lauterbachii within the larger family of Rutaceae and the specific genus Atalantia.
uses: Atalantia lauterbachii is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Pacific Islands. Its leaves are valued for their antimicrobial properties and are used to treat various health issues. Atalantia lauterbachii is also known to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of certain diseases. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential in treating infections and other health conditions. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.

intro: Atalantia lauterbachii, also known as the "Sea Pomegranate", is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory and cardiovascular diseases. The bioactive constituents of Atalantia lauterbachii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and limonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into ointments and salves for topical applications.
